title: In the West Bank, Israeli settlers who commit crimes enjoy total impunity
subhead: Since 2020, Israeli courts have not handed down any jail sentences to Jewish settlers found responsible for the deaths of Palestinian civilians, even as both the number and severity of the crimes they commit have continued to increase.

title: Trump says 'no sticking points' left for a peace deal with Iran

How Trump learned about the scale of Israel's devastating April 8 strikes on Lebanon
subhead: Emmanuel Macron, who wanted Lebanon to be included in the ceasefire reached in the war with Iran, informed the US president in a phone call of the scale of the deadly strikes on Beirut.

title: 'By firing a publisher, Bolloré united writers, who are usually unpredictable and solitary'
subhead: The dismissal of Olivier Nora from the Grasset publishing house has led to the departure of more than 100 writers. There is no known precedent for such an event, observes Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin.

title: Saint-Denis Mayor Bally Bagayoko: 'When you win Paris's largest suburb, it gives you a different standing'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Bally Bagayoko says he can play a role in uniting the left around La France Insoumise leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon for the 2027 French presidential election.

title: Ireland, striving to become Europe's data center hub, falls victim to its own success
subhead: Despite major pressure on the power grid and the risk of rising greenhouse gas emissions, Dublin wants to keep building these facilities for tech giants and establish itself as a European leader in the sector.

subhead: Pending the July 7 court decision that will determine whether Marine Le Pen can stand in elections, the far-right party must prepare for the 2027 presidential campaign without knowing whether she or Jordan Bardella will be its candidate.

title: Virginie Despentes and over 130 writers quit publishing house after Bolloré fires CEO
subhead: More than 130 writers said they would be leaving the Hachette Livre group after billionaire Vincent Bolloré fired Olivier Nora, the publisher of the Grasset house.
